perturbation.original_text: That all information, data, and materials furnished , either orally or otherwise by LSUK shall be considered "Confidential Information ," which includes all technical and non -technical info rmation concerning the past, present, and future business p ractices and/ or plans to include , without limitation, LSUK 's highly proprietary automated print on demand book manufacturing process , its processes, features, functions, performance, components, s ubsystems, use, technology (whether owned or licensed), intellectual property, resources, research, innovations, products or service offerings, strategic partners, techniques or processes, software, patent applications, inventions, trade secrets, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, strategies, or studies and all tangible and intangible property of any kind, whether conveyed in writing or orally by LSUK or its representatives to Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: That certain information, data, and materials furnished, specifically those marked in writing as 'Confidential,' by LSUK shall be considered "Confidential Information," which includes only explicitly labeled technical information directly relating to the current business relationship. Any other information, whether oral or written, will not be considered confidential.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Confidential Information' is broad, encompassing all information furnished by LSUK. The modified definition narrows it significantly, requiring it to be explicitly marked in writing as 'Confidential' and only include explicitly labeled technical information directly relating to the current business relationship. This contradicts the original broad definition of 'Confidential Information', which is in contradiction in the beginning of the file where it states: 'That all information, data, and materials furnished , either orally or otherwise by LSUK shall be considered 'Confidential Information''.
perturbation.contradicted_text: That all information, data, and materials furnished , either orally or otherwise by LSUK shall be considered "Confidential Information ," which includes all technical and non -technical info rmation concerning the past, present, and future business p ractices and/ or plans to include , without limitation, LSUK 's highly proprietary automated print on demand book manufacturing process , its processes, features, functions, performance, components, s ubsystems, use, technology (whether owned or licensed), intellectual property, resources, research, innovations, products or service offerings, strategic partners, techniques or processes, software, patent applications, inventions, trade secrets, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, strategies, or studies and all tangible and intangible property of any kind, whether conveyed in writing or orally by LSUK or its representatives to Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' by requiring it to be explicitly marked as 'Confidential' and directly related to the current business relationship. This contradicts the original text and the contradicted text, which defines 'Confidential Information' broadly as 'all information, data, and materials furnished, either orally or otherwise by LSUK.' The change creates a direct conflict by limiting what is considered confidential, thus creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient agrees not to copy, duplicate, disclose or deliver all or any portion of the Confidential Information to a third party or permit any other third party to inspect, copy or duplicate the same except those parties deemed necessary by the undersi gned to evaluate a possible business relationship (including agents, advisors, affiliates, accountants, attorneys, consultants, and lenders).
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient agrees to copy, duplicate, disclose or deliver all or any portion of the Confidential Information to a third party, if the information is deemed necessary by the Recipient. The Recipient can permit any other third party to inspect, copy or duplicate the same without limitations.
perturbation.explanation: The original text restricts the Recipient from disclosing Confidential Information to third parties, except for specific parties needed to evaluate the business relationship. The changed text permits the Recipient to disclose the Confidential Information to any third party and lift the restrictions stated on the original text, contradicting the original terms, stated previously in the section, regarding the confidentiality of the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient agrees not to copy, duplicate, disclose or deliver all or any portion of the Confidential Information to a third party or permit any other third party to inspect, copy or duplicate the same except those parties deemed necessary by the undersi gned to evaluate a possible business relationship (including agents, advisors, affiliates, accountants, attorneys, consultants, and lenders).
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text (which is also the original text). The original text restricts the recipient from disclosing confidential information. The changed text permits the recipient to disclose confidential information to any third party without limitation. This is a direct conflict, as the 'changed text' now allows something explicitly prohibited in the 'contradicted text'. The perturbation explanation confirms this conflict. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: It is understood that the undersigned may disclose Confidential Information only to parties who (i) require such m aterial for the purpose related to this agreement and (ii) are informed by Recipient of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and agree to be bound by the terms hereof. The Recipient further agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by parties in the preceding sentence, and that these parties will not use any of the Confidential Information for any reason or purpose other than to evaluate a possible business relationship and not in any way detrimental to LSUK .
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to any parties, regardless of their need for the material or their agreement to be bound by the terms. The Recipient is not responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any party, and these parties may use the Confidential Information for any reason or purpose, including in a way that is detrimental to LSUK.
perturbation.explanation: The original text imposes specific conditions for disclosing Confidential Information to other parties. The changed text completely negates these conditions, allowing unrestricted disclosure and removing the Recipient's responsibility for breaches. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the responsibility of the Recipient, with what was stated originally in the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is understood that the undersigned may disclose Confidential Information only to parties who (i) require such m aterial for the purpose related to this agreement and (ii) are informed by Recipient of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and agree to be bound by the terms hereof. The Recipient further agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by parties in the preceding sentence, and that these parties will not use any of the Confidential Information for any reason or purpose other than to evaluate a possible business relationship and not in any way detrimental to LSUK .
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text, and also the original text (they are the same in this case). The original text (and contradicted text) specifies strict conditions for disclosing Confidential Information, emphasizing the recipient's responsibility for breaches. The changed text completely negates these conditions by allowing unrestricted disclosure and removing the recipient's liability for breaches, thereby creating a significant contradiction regarding the responsibilities and restrictions related to confidential information, and satisfying the given definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in perpetually and upon request, Recipient will promptly return all data and materials furnished by LSUK , or provide written certification of its destruction and destroy any internal analyses and/or work papers related to these discussions .
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall terminate immediately upon the first disclosure of confidential information by the Recipient. The Recipient is under no obligation to return or destroy any data or materials furnished by LSUK.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the agreement remains in effect perpetually, with an obligation for the Recipient to return or destroy confidential materials upon request. The modified text states the agreement terminates upon any disclosure, releasing the Recipient from the obligation to return or destroy materials which contradicts the perpetual nature of the agreement and the data destruction obligation in the previous section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall remain in perpetually and upon request, Recipient will promptly return all data and materials furnished by LSUK , or provide written certification of its destruction and destroy any internal analyses and/or work papers related to these discussions .
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction because it states the agreement terminates upon disclosure and that the Recipient is under no obligation to return or destroy materials. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states the agreement remains in effect perpetually and that upon request, the Recipient will return or destroy materials. The contradiction lies in the termination clause and the elimination of the obligation to return or destroy materials, making the two sections mutually exclusive.

perturbation.original_text: Company acknowledges and agrees that any breach of any provisions hereof by Recipient will cause irreparable harm and damage to LSUK . Money damages may not be an adequate remedy and in such event, LSUK , may in addition to other equitable and legal relief that may be available, seek the entry of injunctive relief by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: Company acknowledges and agrees that no breach of any provisions hereof by Recipient will cause any harm or damage to LSUK. Money damages are always an adequate remedy and in such event, LSUK is not entitled to seek injunctive relief by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: The original text asserts that any breach will cause irreparable harm and allows LSUK to seek injunctive relief. The changed text denies any harm from a breach and prevents LSUK from seeking injunctive relief. This contradicts the original statement regarding the potential harm and available legal recourse.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company acknowledges and agrees that any breach of any provisions hereof by Recipient will cause irreparable harm and damage to LSUK . Money damages may not be an adequate remedy and in such event, LSUK , may in addition to other equitable and legal relief that may be available, seek the entry of injunctive relief by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original/contradicted text states that any breach will cause irreparable harm and LSUK may seek injunctive relief. The changed text states that no breach will cause any harm and LSUK is not entitled to seek injunctive relief. This creates a clear and direct conflict within the document regarding the consequences of a breach and the available legal recourse.

perturbation.original_text: In the event a legal dispute arises concerning this Agreement, the prevailing Party shall recover all court costs, expenses, and reasonable attorney's fees.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event a legal dispute arises concerning this Agreement, each party shall bear its own court costs, expenses, and attorney's fees, regardless of who prevails.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause dictates that the winner of a dispute recovers costs. The changed clause states that each side pays their own costs no matter the outcome. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding financial responsibility for legal disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event a legal dispute arises concerning this Agreement, the prevailing Party shall recover all court costs, expenses, and reasonable attorney's fees.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the prevailing party in a legal dispute recovers all court costs and attorney's fees. The changed text states that each party bears their own costs regardless of who prevails. This creates a direct contradiction regarding financial responsibility for legal disputes, as the changed text negates the original text's allocation of costs to the prevailing party, and the contradicted text repeats the initial allocation.
